entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a global crop grown in tropical and subtropical regions and the host for various RNA viruses

entities.name: WESTERN KENYA
entities.description: The region where groundnut leaf samples were collected for the study

entities.name: RNA VIRUSES
entities.description: Pathogens that are major constraints to groundnut production and the focus of the identification study

entities.name: CAULIFLOWER MOSAIC VIRUS
entities.description: A virus newly detected as infecting groundnut hosts

entities.name: COWPEA POLEROVIRUS 2
entities.description: A virus detected in groundnut hosts, reported for the second time globally

entities.name: GROUNDNUT ROSETTE ASSISTOR VIRUS
entities.description: A virus identified in groundnut samples through bioinformatics analysis

entities.name: GROUNDNUT ROSETTE VIRUS
entities.description: A virus identified in groundnut samples through bioinformatics analysis

entities.name: GROUNDNUT ROSETTE VIRUS SATELLITE RNA
entities.description: A satellite RNA associated with groundnut rosette virus identified in samples

entities.name: PEANUT MOTTLE VIRUS
entities.description: A seed-borne virus detected in groundnut with potential origin from Malawi

entities.name: META-TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The overall approach used for the identification of RNA viruses in groundnut

entities.name: TOTAL RNA EXTRACTION
entities.description: The process of extracting total RNA from groundnut leaf samples

entities.name: FIRST-STRAND CDNA SYNTHESIS
entities.description: The process of synthesizing cDNA from extracted RNA for sequencing

entities.name: ILLUMINA HISEQ 2500
entities.description: The sequencing platform used to generate viral RNA sequences

entities.name: DE NOVO ASSEMBLY
entities.description: The process of assembling high-quality viral RNA sequences without a reference genome

entities.name: NCBI VIRUS DATABASE
entities.description: The publicly available database used for the annotation of viral genomes

entities.name: MULTIPLE SEQUENCE ALIGNMENT
entities.description: A method used to align viral sequences for comparative analysis

entities.name: PHYLOGENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analysis used to verify the identity of detected viruses and their evolutionary relationships

entities.name: MEGA X
entities.description: The software used to perform multiple sequence alignment and phylogenetic analysis

entities.name: BIOINFORMATICS ANALYSES
entities.description: Computational analyses used to yield complete and partial viral genomes from sequencing reads

entities.name: NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: High-throughput sequencing technology used for the discovery and identification of novel viruses

entities.name: SEED-BORNE
entities.description: The characteristic of a virus being transmitted through seeds, as seen in PeMoV

entities.name: MALAWI
entities.description: The potential geographical origin of the detected Peanut mottle virus

entities.name: SEED CERTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of ensuring seed health to prevent the spread of seed-borne viruses

entities.name: CROSS-BOUNDARY SEED HEALTH TESTING
entities.description: The practice of testing seeds across borders to maintain seed health

entities.name: VIRUS DISEASES
entities.description: The pathological state in groundnuts caused by sole or mixed infections of viruses
